title: Thread-log skill — construction and design history
charter: The construction and design of the thread-log skill itself: sessions where the skill was first conceived, designed, and built — capturing the original problem (scattered AI session context across tools like Claude, ChatGPT, OpenCode), the architectural decisions (event-sourcing model, two-phase pipeline, slim runtime, gather-then-synthesize extraction), and the evolution of the skill's structure. In scope: skill creation and design sessions, architectural decisions shaping thread-log, design of dependent session skills (claude-code-sessions, opencode-sessions) when they inform thread-log's architecture, and the motivation/vision for where thread-log could go. Out of scope: usage-run friction and process improvements (thread-log-usage covers that), and domain work from the threads thread-log created.
